titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Ed and Barbara Emberley Papers
titlestmt.author: Alison Fulmer

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 16659839.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday June 11, 2025 at 23:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection contains artwork, woodcut and other art prints, correspondence, publicity material, printing woodblocks, photographic prints, sound recordings, born-digital material and other papers created by or related to the work of noted children's book illustrator Ed Emberley and author Barbara Emberley.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 58, 59, and 60: Restricted fragile material including audiovisual media and digital media. Digital surrogates can be made available for use. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged in six series: Series I. Published children's books, 1961-2008; Series II. Projects and printed material, 1958-2014; Series III. Unpublished material, circa 1960-1969; Series IV. Early work, circa 1950-1961, 1971; Series V. Professional papers, 1963-2016; Series VI. Personal papers, 1950-1956, 1999-2005.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection contains artwork, woodcut and other art prints, correspondence, publicity material, printing woodblocks, photographic prints, sound recordings, born-digital material and other papers created by or related to the work of noted children's book illustrator Ed Emberley and author Barbara Emberley. Of note is production art for the Emberley's children's books in Series I, including Drummer Hoff , for which Ed Emberley was awarded the Caldecott Medal.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Ed and Barbara Emberley papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns.

bioghist.head: Biographical Note
bioghist.p: Edward Randolph Emberley was born in Malden, Massachusetts on October 19, 1931. He attended the Massachusetts College of Art in Boston where he received a Bachelor of Fine Arts degree in painting and illustration. After serving in the United States Army for two years, he continued his studies at the Rhode Island School of Design for one year.

Emberley was employed in the 1950's as a pasteup artist and cartoonist for a direct mail advertising firm in Boston, Massachusetts and then moved into freelance illustrating. He wrote and illustrated his first book, The Wing on a Flea , which was published in 1961.

Barbara Anne Emberley (née Collins) was born in Chicago, Illinois on December 12, 1932. She attended the Massachusetts College of Art where she received a Bachelor of Fine Arts. Barbara and Ed met while at college and married in 1955. Barbara worked as a librarian at Brown University while Ed completed his formal art training at the Rhode Island School of Design. Barbara is a children's book author.

The Emberleys have collaborated on a number of children's books, including such titles as the Caldecott award-winning Drummer Hoff , Night's Nice , Simon's Song , and One Wide River to Cross . The couple has two children, Rebecca and Michael, who are both children's book authors and illustrators. Ed and Barbara live in Ipswich, Massachusetts where they run a private hand press, Bird in Bush Press.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Cumberland Rare Books on the Jockey Hollow Fund, 2023.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ed and Barbara Emberley papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog for "From the Ed and Barbara Emberley Papers".

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Archival Description Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains production art, book dummies, print proofs, and other material relating to the production of Ed and Barbara Emberley's children's story books and science books. Much of the production art in this series includes acetate color separations- transparent film sheets used to indicate the placement of color in the printing process.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by title. The publication year for each book is listed in parentheses after the title.
Ed Emberley's 1985 series of books about computers are grouped together under the heading "Computer Series", reflecting the original order of these materials.

did.unittitle: Book dummies with original art and dust jacket
Birds Eat and Eat and Eat (1963)
Book dummy with original art- first draft
Book dummy with original art- second draft
Woodblock print proofs of goat and lion
Woodblock print proof of gorilla and balsa woodblock

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains 13 woodblocks created by Ed Emberley for Cock-a-Doodle-Doo . The woodblocks include images of a mosquito, chick, bee, duck, rooster, lion, mouse, goat, crocodile, sheep and butterfly.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These items are not currently available for use due to conservation treatment.

did.unittitle: Book dummy of print proofs and dust jacket
Dust jacket production art with color separations and print proofs
Dust jacket production art with color separations and print proofs
How to Talk to Your Computer (1985)
Dust jacket production art with color separations
Miscellaneous production art and promotional images
Dust jacket production art with color separations
Dust jacket production art with color separations
Book dummy and dust jacket of print proofs
Book dummy of print proofs in blue
Filmstrip images of Drummer Hoff produced by Weston Woods

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This filmestrip was produced in conjunction with material in the folder "Sound recordings of a reading of Drummer Hoff produced by Weston Woods, 1968".

did.unittitle: "Gift record" of a reading of Drummer Hoff produced by Weston Woods

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains two flexible 7 inch "gift records", commemorative artifacts containing audio of a reading of Drummer Hoff . These records were produced in conjunction with material in the folder "Filmstrip images of Drummer Hoff produced by Weston Woods, 1968".

did.unittitle: Original color drawings - possible pages from a book dummy
Original sketch, woodblock print proof, and final proof of man with powder keg
Production art- black and white print proofs
Printed material relating to Ed Emberley's Caldecott Award for Drummer Hoff
Sound recording of "Barbara and Ed Emberley talking about Drummer Hoff "

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a commercial sound recording on a 7" phonograph record (33 1/3 rpm) produced by Young Readers Press.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59: Restricted fragile audiovisual material. A reference copy may be requested. Consult Public Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Sound recording of "Ed Emberley Acceptance Speech and Drummer Hoff "

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a sound recording on 1/4" audio tape on a 7" open reel.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: A transcript of Ed Emberley's acceptance speech can be found in The Horn Book Magazine : https://www.hbook.com/story/caldecott-award-acceptance-by-ed-emberley.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 60: Restricted fragile audiovisual material. A reference copy may be requested. Consult Public Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous production art pages with acetate separations
Book dummy of print proofs and dust jacket
Production art with color separations and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains production art associated with color separations housed in box 47.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain color separations associated with production art housed in box 38.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains material from the 1963 edition of Night's Nice .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains material from the 2008 edition of Night's Nice .

did.unittitle: Dust jacket production art with color separations
Cover art, balsa woodcut, and book dummy mounted on board
Dust jacket production art with color separations
Original art, sketches, and book dummy on board
Dust jacket production art with color separations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains material from the 1961 edition of The Wing on a Flea.

did.unittitle: Dust jacket, reviews, and activity sheet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains material from the 2001 edition of The Wing on a Flea.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Production art for this publication is arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Book dummy of print proofs, editor's notes, and miscellaneous proofs
Production art- dust jacket print proofs
Production art- print proofs with edits
Production art- print proofs from copyeditor
Production art- print proofs with note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains material from the 1965 edition of Yankee Doodle.

did.unittitle: Calendar and publisher's catalog featuring Yankee Doodle art
Dust jacket mock up and print proof
Production note and woodblock print proof

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains material from the 1994 edition of Yankee Doodle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes production art, proofs, final products, and correspondence relating to Ed and Barbara Emberley's other printing and publishing projects. These projects resulted in activity sheets, art prints, illustrations, grade school "reader" books, and print ephemera such as bookmarks, greeting cards, pamphlets, and posters.
For more project files, see Series V: Professional papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged in six subseries: Activity sheets and books, Bird in Bush Press printed material, Children's "reader" books, Library and literacy projects, Other projects, and Periodical projects. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by title unless noted otherwise.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes material from projects featuring Ed Emberley's art and one activity sheet created by Barbara Emberley.

did.unittitle: Fearon Teacher Aids: The Ed Emberley Collection and other publications by Will Howell
Fun with Soap Sculpture print proofs
KidVidz Squiggles, Dots, and Lines press packet
Learning to Write With Ed Emberley production art
Puppet activity sheet by Barbara Emberley
Bird in Bush Press printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain printed ephemera such as greeting cards, flyers, pamphlets, stationery, invitations, and business cards created by Ed and Barbara Emberley with their private hand press, Bird in Bush Press.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains production art and proofs for grade school reader books featuring Ed Emberley's art.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material in this subseries is arranged by publisher and then alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: The Racoon and Mrs. McGinnis print proofs
The Racoon and Mrs. McGinnis production art with original drawings
The Book That Saved Earth print proofs
High, Wide, and Handsome print proofs
High, Wide, and Handsome production art with original drawings
The Wolf and the Fox print proofs
In the Dark of the Night print proof
Pencil Magic/The Magic Pencil print proofs
Pencil Magic/The Magic Pencil production art with original drawings
The Three Billy Goats print proofs
Untitled production art- possibly The Mitten

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes material created for libraries, reading programs, and other literacy projects featuring Ed Emberley's art.

did.unittitle: Central and Western Massachusetts Library System summer reading poster
The Children's Book Council "Read and Laugh" poster
Ipswich Public Library logo project and events: correspondence and proofs
Milwaukee Public Library "Books for Everyone" flyer
Milwaukee Public Library "Books for Everyone" poster
New England Booksellers Association Book Review Contest: poster and printed material
Summer reading printed material and original sketches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes material from projects featuring Ed Emberley's art.

did.unittitle: Advertisement for gas cooking party at Symphony Hall
Crowell Crocodile advertisement for publisher Thomas Crowell
Greeting cards for the University of Southern Mississippi Collection of Children's Literature

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain printouts of digital art, notes, hypermedia instruction guides, and correspondence between Ed Emberley and Mary Anderson about a HyperCard software project featuring Emberley's digital art of monkeys and other animals. Four 3.5" floppy disks containing born-digital project files were removed from folder 20 and placed in Box 58.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four 3.5" floppy disks containing the project files for Emberley's HyperCard project. These floppy disks were removed from Box 12 folder 20.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 58: Restricted fragile computer media. Original computer media may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies instead of the originals.

did.physdesc: 819.20 Kilobytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.
819.20 Kilobytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.
819.20 Kilobytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.
819.20 Kilobytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.

did.unittitle: Kidstamps t-shirt and stamp designs and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains black and white and color print proofs of Ed Emberley's woodblock designs including images from Punch and Judy and Cock-a-Doodle Doo and miscellaneous images of animals and figures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains poster advertisements for Noye's Fludde , Benjamin Britten's musical setting of the story of Noah's ark, performed at the First Congregational Church in Geenwich, Connecticut on November 6, 2005.

did.unittitle: Sketches: self-portrait, greeting card, frog, and caricature

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ed Emberley created this sign in support of wetland and floodplain protection in Ipswich, Massachusetts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes production art and printed material from Ed Emberley's projects for periodical publications, such as magazines and newspapers. Emberley's art appeared in educational, family, religious, and art publications as well as the regional newspaper Reunion , an African American newspaper based in Sudbury, MA.

did.unittitle: Family Fun magazine: "8 Things Anyone Can Draw"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Friend is a publication of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints.

did.unittitle: The Friend magazine- "Daddy Longlegs" production art
The Friend magazine- "Sara's Magical Land" production art
The Friend magazine- "A Tunnel is for Digging" production art with color separations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder includes publications from Light and Life Press including Covenant: Youth Today and Light and Life Evangel as well as printed pamphlets.

did.unittitle: Reunion newspaper drawing activity pages featuring Ed Emberley's art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains photocopies of Ed Emberley's drawing activity pages that were published in Reunion newspaper. Reunion is a monthly publication focused on the "lifestyles, news, events, and thinking of African Americans living, working, and being educated in the New England region." Reunion was published in Sudbury, MA and ran through the 1990s. The first issue was published in October 1992.

did.unittitle: Scholastic magazine- The Story of Paul Bunyan adaptation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains production art, sketches, book dummies, and other material created by Ed Emberley for unpublished works.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by title. Flip books are grouped together.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Story by Evelyn Good Pearson with illustrations by Ed Emberley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most flip books are untitled, except for "How to Make a Six". The titles listed for flip books are derived from images in the book.

did.unittitle: Blank flip book dummies box set
Flip book planning sketches, notes, and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contains flip books collected by Ed Emberley and published or authored by others.

Jonah book dummy with original art
The Marvelous Bouncing Egg production art

scopecontent.p: Story by Joe Keating with illustrations by Ed Emberley.

scopecontent.p: Story by Betty Boegehold.

"Three Bears" story production art with photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This production art on board features a color painting on a man in fancy attire walking away from a castle among villagers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This production art on board features a color painting of a family in a loaded farm truck driving past a farm house.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Story by John G. Keller with illustrations by Ed Emberley. It is possible that this cover may be early concept art for Krispin's Fair .

did.unittitle: Who Said Boo? book dummy with annotations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains art created by Ed Emberley while he was a student at the Massachusetts School of Art in Boston (now called the Massachusetts College of Art and Design or MassArt) and his early freelance work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by subject or format.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain artwork Ed Emberley produced while at Massachusetts School of Art in Boston (Class of 1954).

did.unittitle: Art school material- figure sketches and color study

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain artwork Ed Emberley produced while at Massachusetts School of Art in Boston (Class of 1954).

did.unittitle: Color illustrations on paper and board

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Based on the original arrangement of these materials, it is unclear if these drawings and paintings are Ed Emberley's school work or early freelance work.

did.unittitle: Ink sketches of figures and caricatures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Based on the original arrangement of these materials, it is unclear if these sketches are Ed Emberley's school work or early freelance work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Based on the original arrangement of these materials, it is unclear if these prints are Ed Emberley's school work or early freelance work.

did.unittitle: Prints of caricatures and other illustrations
Rubylith of a person on horseback
Sketch of a woman and baby (1961) and drawing of a flower (1971)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five small sketchbooks containing pencil and pen and ink sketches of figures, caricatures, and animals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains pen and ink sketches of figures, caricatures, and animals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two sketchbooks containing pencil and pen and ink sketches of figures, caricatures, objects, and animals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder contains stencils of geometric and leaf-shaped motifs.

did.unittitle: Watercolor portrait of a young man
Woodblock mounted on burlap depicting a map with buildings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This thin woodcut is mounted on burlap fabric. The woodcut depicts a stylized map featuring buildings, roads, and a train track.

did.unittitle: Woodblock mounted on burlap depicting a cat on a chair

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This thin woodcut is mounted on burlap fabric. The woodcut depicts a cat curled up on a chair. The corresponding woodblock prints and sketch are located in Box 35, "Woodblock prints and sketch of a cat on a chair".

did.unittitle: Woodblock prints and sketch of a cat on a chair

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The corresponding woodblock for these prints and sketch is located in Box 54, "Woodblock mounted on burlap depicting a cat on a chair".

did.unittitle: Woodblock prints of figures and a truck

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Based on the original arrangement of these materials, it is unclear if these prints are Ed Emberley's school work or early freelance work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, press clippings, event material, writings, photographs, and other printed material relating to Ed and Barbara Emberley's work. Business correspondence contains some project files for Ed Emberley's work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged in eleven subseries: Awards, Bibliographic and biographical material, Business correspondence, Event and exhibition material, Fan correspondence, Legal files, Photographs, Press files, Project files, Publications, Writings by Ed Emberley. Material in each subseries is arranged alphabetically unless noted otherwise.

did.unittitle: Awards from University of Southern Mississippi
Ed Emberley cover letters and description of his programs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes Ed and Barbara Emberley's correspondence with individuals and businesses regarding various publishing projects. For more material relating to their projects, see Series II: Projects and printed material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This subseries is arranged alphabetically by surname, business, or subject.

did.unittitle: Robert Dornish- Reviews of Three: An Emberley Family Sketch Book
"It's the Parents' Choice"- a review of children's media
Lakeshore Learning Materials- Go Away Big Green Monster activity kit
Robert Morris- "Ed Emberley's Thumbprint Fun" in Nick Jr. Magazine
Simon and Schuster- The Story of Paul Bunyan and Yankee Doodle
Thank you notes from Ed Emberley
Mo Willems postcard to Barbara Emberley
Gay Young- "Ed Emberley's Drawing Club Online"
Joyce Audi Zarins- digital art project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains postcards, flyers, booklets, advertisements, and correspondence pertaining to exhibitions and events featuring the work of Ed, Barbara, Rebecca, and Michael Emberley. There are also chalk drawings that Ed Emberley created during his Chalk Talk events.

did.unittitle: Chalk Talk and book signing event
Ed Emberley art event in Japan
Emberley family art exhibits and events ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder includes fan drawings of the ship, the Sea Hawk, from Ed Emberley's Big Purple Drawing Book.

did.unittitle: Fan correspondence- St. Thomas More School, Spokane, Washington
Agent contract for Picture Pie - unsigned document
Ed Emberley assignment of rights to Barbara Emberley- unsigned document
Photographs of Ed Emberley and school visits

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains newspaper clippings, magazine articles, print outs, book fair and book conference programs, and ephemera relating to Ed, Barbara, Rebecca, and Michael Emberley's work and events.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous project files, correspondence, and articles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains publications referencing Ed Emberley and publisher catalogs featuring Ed and Barbara Emberley's work.

title.part: Artful Scribbles: The Significance of Children's Drawings
Emberley Galaxy: A Tribute to Ed Emberley

did.unittitle: "The Great Copy Monster" drafts and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Ed Emberley's personal correspondence and printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This address book was originally located with material in Series IV: Early work.
